title: chrysanthemum alpinum var. minimum
intro: Chrysanthemum alpinum var. minimum, also known as the dwarf alpine chrysanthemum, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum alpinum var. minimum include reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and soothing respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
